name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: Ria Money Transfer is a global money transfer service enabling customers to send and receive funds across 190+ countries through multiple channels including online, mobile app, in-person locations, and partnerships with retailers like 7-Eleven.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer operates as an international remittance and money transfer provider with a distributed agent network across the United States and globally. The company facilitates cross-border payments and domestic money movement through both digital and physical channels, partnering with convenience stores, check cashers, pharmacies, and small service businesses to extend their reach.

Their service offerings span multiple sending methods (online, mobile app, in-person, WhatsApp) and receiving options (bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, ATM withdrawal). Ria supports transfers to over 190 countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania. They provide supplementary financial tools including a currency converter and IBAN calculator, positioning themselves as a comprehensive remittance platform rather than a single-channel provider.

Ria's distribution model distinguishes them through agent partnerships with established retailers. Their locations include 24-hour 7-Eleven branches, check cashing outlets like PLS Check Cashers, and independent multiservice businesses, enabling cash pickups and same-location funding. This hybrid digital-and-physical approach serves unbanked and underbanked populations who may lack access to traditional banking or prefer cash transactions.

However, the website content provided contains no information about exchange rates, fees, transfer speeds, regulatory compliance details, or customer service quality. The location list shows primarily New York City area agents, limiting visibility into nationwide coverage. No independent verification of service quality, security certifications, or customer complaint data is available from this website content alone.
pros: Multiple sending methods including WhatsApp, online, mobile app, and in-person options for flexibility
Extensive receiving options (bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, ATM withdrawal) accommodating various preferences
Global reach to 190+ countries across six continents
24-hour availability at 7-Eleven partner locations for time-flexible access
Established agent network including convenient retail partnerships (pharmacies, grocery stores, check cashers)
Mobile app for on-the-go money transfers
Supplementary tools like currency converter and IBAN calculator
cons: Website provides no fee structure, exchange rate information, or transfer speed details for consumer comparison
No visible customer service contact information, complaints resolution process, or satisfaction guarantees on the location page
Limited regulatory or compliance transparency (no mention of licensing, OFAC compliance, or consumer protections)
Agent network appears concentrated in specific urban areas based on example locations; nationwide coverage unclear
No pricing comparison tool or rate quotes available directly from the location finder interface
best_for: Immigrants and expatriates sending regular remittances to family in other countries
Unbanked and underbanked consumers who prefer cash transactions over bank accounts
Users seeking 24-hour money transfer access through convenient retail locations
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best suited for individuals who regularly send money internationally or domestically and value flexible receiving options and extended hours of operation. The main caveat is that critical financial details—fees, exchange rates, and transfer speeds—are absent from their web presence, requiring direct contact with agents or the company to make informed comparisons with competitors.
services: In-person money transfer at agent locations
